prompt: Implement a CivicEval Governance Portal for offline, on-premise democratic evaluations and performance-linked project publishing. The system serves four roles: System Administrator (platform configuration and security), Program Owner (creates activities and manages content), Reviewer/Moderator (content and comment governance), and Participant (registers, checks in, reads, and evaluates). The Angular web UI provides a desktop-first workspace with a left navigation for Activities, Content Library, Rankings, and Analytics. Program Owners create an evaluation activity with a defined participation scope (individuals or teams), a registration window (e.g., 03/25/2026 9:00 AM–04/10/2026 5:00 PM), and an optional on-site check-in mode that generates a one-time local check-in code displayed on a kiosk screen and manually entered by participants to record attendance without any SMS/email. Activities use customizable evaluation forms with required questions, numeric scales (default 1–5), and comment fields with a 500-character limit; participants see immediate validation feedback and a confirmation receipt number after submission. The CMS supports rich-text project pages with drag-and-drop media upload (images/videos/PDF credentials), draft-to-publish controls, version history with rollback, and moderation for comments/Q&A including approve, pin, block, and report handling. A Ranking view lets authorized users compare submitted projects using a composite scoring model that blends benchmark score, price in USD, and volatility ranges, with user-defined weights (must sum to 100%) and an explain-why panel that breaks down the contribution of each factor. The Analytics dashboard shows PV/UV, read completion rate, dwell time, search conversion, content popularity, and traffic sources for any custom date range, with CSV export for audits and leadership reviews. The backend uses Fastify to expose REST-style APIs over TLS within the local network, with PostgreSQL as the system of record for accounts, roles, activities, evaluation submissions, content versions, and analytics events. Authentication is strictly local username + password, enforcing at least 12 characters with complexity rules and a 15-minute lockout after 5 failed attempts; sessions expire after 30 minutes of inactivity and abnormal-login policies flag new-device sign-ins for admin review. API requests require signed tokens plus anti-replay nonces and timestamps (reject if older than 60 seconds), and rate limiting defaults to 60 requests/minute/user with additional throttles on login and search to deter bots. Data governance includes a maintained data dictionary, quality validation (required fields, range checks), duplicate remediation rules for repeated registrations, immutable change history for evaluations, sensitive-field masking (e.g., show only last 4 of employee ID), and access tracking for every export and permission change; audit logs for critical actions are retained for 7 years. Content safety scanning is offline and rule-based: uploads are restricted to approved formats, max 250 MB per file, hashed for fingerprinting, and checked against a configurable sensitive-word list before publish; assets are stored on local disks with private read, expiring share links (default 7 days), and visible watermarking on downloaded PDFs/images for traceability. Encryption at rest is required for database and file volumes, and nightly backups include PostgreSQL dumps plus asset snapshots with 30-day retention and a documented restore drill performed quarterly, ensuring the entire system remains fully functional without internet dependencies.
